protocol.name: wlr_layer_shell
protocol.copyright:
Copyright © 2017 Christopher Walber Copyright © 2017 Simon Ser Copyright © 2019 Scott Anderson
Permission to use, copy, modify, distribute, and sell this software and its documentation for any purpose is hereby granted without fee, provided that the above copyright notice appear in all copies and that both that copyright notice and this permission notice appear in all supporting documentation. No representations are made about the suitability of this software for any purpose. It is provided "as is" without express or implied warranty.

interface.name: zwlr_layer_shell_v1

description.summary: create surfaces that are positioned relative to other surfaces

interface.description:
Clients can use this interface to assign the surface_manager role to wl_surfaces. Such surfaces exist in a layer above the normal surface managed by the compositor.
Layer surfaces can be given a keyboard interactivity mode, and can be positioned in a layer, anchored, given exclusive-zone geometry, and given a margin. Together, these properties determine the surface's position on the screen.

event.name: destroy

description.summary: the compositor has finished with the layer_shell

event.description: This event indicates that the compositor is done sending manager events. The compositor will destroy the object immediately after sending this event, so it is not safe to send any more requests after this point.

request.name: get_layer_surface
request.description: Create a new layer surface. The surface is not mapped until it is given a layer, anchor, and exclusive zone. This creates an zwlr_layer_surface_v1 object.

interface.name: zwlr_layer_surface_v1

description.summary: layer_surface is positioned relative to other surfaces

interface.description: A layer surface is an interface that allows the client to position a surface relative to other surfaces. This interface is similar to wl_shell_surface in that it provides a way for a client to place a surface on the screen, but it provides more control over the placement.

event.name: destroy

description.summary: the surface has been closed by the compositor

event.description: The compositor has decided that this surface should no longer be visible. This can happen, for example, when the output the surface was displayed on is removed. The surface is unmapped and the client should destroy it.

event.name: configure
event.description: The compositor sends this event when it wants to suggest changes to the surface's position, size, or state. The client can then acknowledge the changes by calling ack_configure.

request.name: ack_configure
request.description: When a configure event is received, the client should acknowledge it by calling this request.

request.name: set_size

description.summary: set the size of the surface

request.description: Set the width and height of the surface. The compositor will send a configure event with the new size.

request.name: set_anchor

description.summary: set the anchor for the surface

request.description: Set what edges the surface is anchored to.

request.name: set_exclusive_zone

description.summary: set the exclusive zone of the surface

request.description: The exclusive zone defines how much of the output is reserved for this surface. The compositor will use this to push other surfaces away.

request.name: set_margin

description.summary: set the margin of the surface

request.description: Set the margin of the surface.

request.name: set_keyboard_interactivity
request.description: Set whether the surface should receive keyboard events.

request.name: get_popup

description.summary: assign a role to a surface

request.description: This request creates a popup for the surface.

event.name: closed
event.description: The compositor has decided that this surface should be closed.
